change_comments.message: sorry, i couldnt complete review today but here are my initial comments
just a small issue with unnecessary casting of Boolean to boolean and a question about the (need for) new testware
all else good but would still leek you to remove that test, se ebelow

inline_comments.message: I think this method is not required. directly use groupingEnabled parameter wherever required
final boolean groupingEnabled = isGroupingEnabled(groupingEnabled); will throw error as its same variable name as in parameters.
Used to avoid sonar warnings of code repetition
do you need to provide 2 examples where grouping is enabled and grouping disabled.
what is the update in the POJO??
NB: can be extracted to a method.
NB: just checking . dont u want to use toDataMapWithIdentifier method that Anupriya modified?? i thought there was some blocking code.
will u also add the integration test with the grouping enabled when this feature is fully added?? if yes then u can create a sub-task and then we can ignore the comment to add the test here.

confused, so target data for create and remove has both key-value pair and array ? 
array for code, key-value for name? 

but for action replace , name is array??

if you intend both to be part of the list "target-data" , maybe remove the comma after 3
    
    
please double check
i see the idea here but this just repeats the code
can just have groupingEnabled here or change the parameter name to 'groupDataNodes'
why remove variable before? easier to read with it before

i dont get 'added'DataNodes 
suggestion: deltaReportDataNodes? or something else
no actual change in file, revert for smaller patch set

these can just be imported no need to create var for it

import static org.onap.cps.api.parameters.FetchDescendantsOption.INCLUDE_ALL_DESCENDANTS
import static org.onap.cps.api.parameters.FetchDescendantsOption.OMIT_DESCENDANTS

for tests that doesnt specify grouping boolean , id create a shared variable at top be used by other tests and use it here 
let groupingDisabled = true
let groupingEnabled = false

We planned to change the leaves from Map<String, Serializable> to Map<String, Object> will be taking it as part of separate patch as the feature is implemented partially in this patch. I am reverting this statement
yes i thought of adding it in Part 2 when all the delta report actions: add, remove and update are implemented.

We planned to change the type of source leaves and target leaves hence to avoid to many changes in planning stage I was using it directly. But I reverted it back as those changes are planned for later stage.
to differentiate from removed and updated deltaReportEntries. Changes for that are in Part 2 of this patch :)
same as above, we will have delta report entries that contain removed and updated data in Part 2 of the patch.
no it was toDataMapForApiV3, I saw she had used a more granular approach in implementing the method and I adjusted my code accordingly
yes, will add it as a sub-task
NB: I would use true as an example

groupDataNodes
OR
groupDataNodesAsPrimitive
(dont vary the main name just because you are converting it, use the 'as....' pattern as suggested here)

NB: add def NO_GROUPING = false
at the top of the file and use it as needed to make it clearer what parameter is what in all tests
use consistent name from the controller onward, I suggest 'groupDataNodes' is the better name
ensure consistent naming, i wont repeat this comment...
is this really needed?! You are checking if the map is empty and then return an empty map , seems silly  and is very confusing to ready anyway
this is not needed. I was testing the overall feature with update and remove operations and forgot to remove this code

I dont think this is needed
(I believe I comment similar somewhere else/different commit)
You can use the Boolean paarmeter directly on line 66, java will correctly convert Boolean to boolean, null will be false
This means you can just rename the "parameter" on line 60 to groupDataNodes and simply remove this line
NB: Since these are primitive 'constants' maybe use upper case

is this realy  different ie covers a different code path then when grouping is disabled?
IF it doesn't cover a unique code path I would NOT add this test (maintenance costs)
yes the code splits to different paths as soon as we check if grouping is enabled or disabled

NB: I understand, but grouping WITH data is a different but there is NO special branching for handling identical outcome. I ran the coverage report without this test and it is stil 100% covered on Delta Impl so no need to add this!
strange that removing this test results in coverage issues. Toine checked on his machine and checked the coverage and branching without this issue and it worked fine.
The coverage did go down by 1% as in the build logs and I cross verified locally so reverted the patch
I tried on my local. It went down for me
yeah. same happening for me. let me check with Toine on this.

OK, my bad I forgot to include the word 'clean' when I ran a mvn test with coverage and now I see the branch INSIDE the condensed report method you need to cover...

Sorry about that....
